item.description: NBC News chief data analyst Steve Kornacki breaks down real-time vote tallies from California, Iowa, New Jersey, Montana, South Dakota and New Mexico, while giving viewers a behind-the-scenes look at election night coverage.

media:content.media:title: Kornacki Cam: Watch Steve analyze midterm primary election results
media:content.media:description: NBC News chief data analyst Steve Kornacki breaks down real-time vote tallies from California, Iowa, New Jersey, Montana, South Dakota and New Mexico, while giving viewers a behind-the-scenes look at election night coverage.

item.title: Kornacki Cam: Watch Steve analyze midterm primary election results

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Four House members have already lost re-election bids so far this year, just a few months into primary season.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Mike Thompson, D-Calif., is among a group of endangered Democratic House members facing serious challengers this year.

item.title: Primary challengers threaten to unseat House members across the country

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The Supreme Court allowed Alabama to use a congressional map that eliminates one of two majority-Black districts in the state in a win for Republicans.

media:content.media:text: People hold signs that say "Black Voter Matter", "It's About Us", and "History Is Watching" while standing outside
media:content.media:description: Several pro-democracy groups protest Alabama's new congressional map in Montgomery on May 4.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use congressional map that eliminates a majority-Black district

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: A man was barricaded with an unknown number of hostages Tuesday at a California bank where police had received reports of a bomb threat.

media:content.media:text: Bakersfield Police Department on scene at the Chase Bank Building in Bakersfield, Calif., on June 2, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Bakersfield Police Department on scene at the Chase Bank Building in Bakersfield, Calif., on Tuesday.
Bakersfield Police Department on scene at the Chase Bank Building in Bakersfield, Calif., on Tuesday.

item.title: Man takes hostages at California bank as bomb threat prompts evacuations

item.description: Centcom says the U.S. military disabled the engine of a ship trying to break through the blockade of Iran's ports. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

media:content.media:title: U.S. targets ship trying to break through blockade
media:content.media:description: Centcom says the U.S. military disabled the engine of a ship trying to break through the blockade of Iran's ports. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

item.title: U.S. targets ship trying to break through blockade

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said Tuesday the Justice Department isn’t moving forward with its $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund.

media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche during a hearing in the Rayburn building on Tuesday.

item.title: Todd Blanche says DOJ ‘not moving forward’ with ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

item.dc:creator: Dan De Luce
Rebecca Shabad
Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

media:content.media:description: Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency Bill Pulte walks outside the White House, Tuesday, Sept. 2, 2025, in Washington.

item.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: “60 Minutes” executive producer Nick Bilton pressed ahead with plans to speak individually with staffers a day after correspondent Scott Pelley confronted him.

media:content.media:description: Nick Bilton onstage at Day 1 of the Vanity Fair New Establishment Summit 2018 at The Wallis Annenberg Center for the Performing Arts on Oct. 9, 2018.
"60 Minutes" correspondent Scott Pelley.

item.title: New ‘60 Minutes’ boss takes meetings with staffers after clash with Scott Pelley

item.description: Hundreds of people gathered in Nanyuki, Kenya, to protest a U.S.-proposed Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus. Kenya does not have any suspected Ebola cases, but neighboring Uganda has reported nine and closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

media:content.media:title: Protests erupt over U.S. Ebola quarantine center in Kenya
media:content.media:description: Hundreds of people gathered in Nanyuki, Kenya, to protest a U.S.-proposed Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus. Kenya does not have any suspected Ebola cases, but neighboring Uganda has reported nine and closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Protests erupt over U.S. Ebola quarantine center in Kenya

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Matt Freese once wrote a college research project on penalty kicks. Now he’s competing to be the starting goalie for the U.S. men’s national team at this summer’s World Cup.

media:content.media:text: United States v Paraguay - International Friendly
New York City FC v Nashville SC

item.title: Meet the Harvard grad who might lead the U.S. to World Cup glory

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Iowa state Rep. Josh Turek has won the Democratic nomination for his state’s open U.S.

media:content.media:text: Political Candidates Crisscross Iowa To Campaign Ahead Of State Primary
media:content.media:description: Democratic Senate candidate Josh Turek during a campaign event in Westside, Iowa on May 29.

media:content.media:text: Iowa Senate Candidate Ashley Hinson Votes On Primary Day
media:content.media:description: Democratic Senate candidate Josh Turek during a campaign event in Westside, Iowa on May 29.

item.title: Josh Turek wins Democratic primary in battleground Iowa Senate race

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The Supreme Court allowed Alabama to use a congressional map that eliminates one of two majority-Black districts in the state in a win for Republicans.

media:content.media:text: People hold signs that say "Black Voter Matter", "It's About Us", and "History Is Watching" while standing outside
media:content.media:description: Several pro-democracy groups protest Alabama's new congressional map in Montgomery on May 4.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use congressional map that eliminates a majority-Black district

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: Former Interior Secretary Deb Haaland has won the Democratic primary in New Mexico’s race for governor, NBC News projects

media:content.media:description: Democrat Deb Haaland would be the first female Native American governor if she is elected this fall.

item.title: Former Interior Secretary Deb Haaland wins Democratic nomination in New Mexico governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Kyle Stewart
item.description: Rebecca Bennett, a former Navy helicopter pilot and healthcare executive, has won the Democratic primary in New Jersey’s 7th Congressional District, NBC News projects, setting up a general election campaign against Republican Rep. Tom Kean Jr.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., has faced scrutiny for his prolonged absence from the U.S. Capitol.

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Bennett meets with constituents at an event.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., has faced scrutiny for his prolonged absence from the U.S. Capitol.

media:content.media:text: Tina Shah in a doctors white coat and scrubs.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., has faced scrutiny for his prolonged absence from the U.S. Capitol.

item.title: Democrat Rebecca Bennett will take on GOP Rep. Tom Kean Jr., who has been absent from Congress

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Brennan Leach
item.description: Graham Platner visited Washington on Tuesday to meet with Democrats amid fresh controversy.

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner stands at a podium
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, Democratic U.S. Senate candidate for Maine, speaks during a Fighting Oligarchy event in Portland, Maine, on May 25, 2026.

item.title: Maine’s Graham Platner meets with Democratic senators amid controversy

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Courtney Kube
item.description: Jan. 6 defendant now works at Pentagon

media:content.media:description: Supporters of President Donald Trump gather outside the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Jan. 6 defendant who said he was ashamed of ‘foolish’ actions now works at Pentagon

item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero
item.description: A man was barricaded with an unknown number of hostages Tuesday at a California bank where police had received reports of a bomb threat.

media:content.media:text: Bakersfield Police Department on scene at the Chase Bank Building in Bakersfield, Calif., on June 2, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Bakersfield Police Department on scene at the Chase Bank Building in Bakersfield, Calif., on Tuesday.
Bakersfield Police Department on scene at the Chase Bank Building in Bakersfield, Calif., on Tuesday.

item.title: Man takes hostages at California bank as bomb threat prompts evacuations

item.dc:creator: Phil Helsel
George Solis
Maya Rosenberg
item.description: U.S. Coast Guard divers have arrived in the Bahamas as the agency resumes its search for Lynette Hooker, the American woman who disappeared in April after, her husband said, she fell from a boat, an official said Tuesday

media:content.media:description: Lynette Hooker.

item.title: Coast Guard divers arrive in Bahamas to search for missing American Lynette Hooker

item.dc:creator: Chloe Melas
Andrew Blankstein
David K. Li
item.description: Los Angeles prosecutors are investigating sexual assault allegations against disgraced artist and music executive Sean “Diddy” Combs, an official said.

media:content.media:text: Sean "Diddy" Combs attends the 2022 Billboard Music Awards.
media:content.media:description: Sean "Diddy" Combs at the 2022 Billboard Music Awards.
Sean "Diddy" Combs at the 2022 Billboard Music Awards.

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors reviewing sex assault allegations against Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Shabad
item.description: Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan’s re-election campaign is accusing one of his opponents in Alaska and Democrats in Washington of recruiting a candidate with the same first and last name: Dan Sullivan.“Mary Peltola and D.C.

media:content.media:description: Sen. Dan Sullivan, R-Alaska, called the move a "blatant attempt to confuse and disenfranchise Alaskans."

item.title: Sen. Dan Sullivan accuses Democrats of recruiting Alaska Senate candidate with the same name

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: A rodeo rider whose horse was stabbed over the weekend in a Las Vegas barn said Tuesday she came “face-to-face” with the teenager charged with attacking her mare and two other horses.

media:content.media:text: A horse stands in an outdoor barn with white ointment covering three wounds and a wrap covering its face
media:content.media:description: South Point Casino in Las Vegas.

media:content.media:text: A horse stands in an outdoor barn with white ointment covering three wounds and a wrape covering its face
media:content.media:description: South Point Casino in Las Vegas.

media:content.media:text: Arielle Phillips rides a horse inside of a stadium
media:content.media:description: South Point Casino in Las Vegas.

item.title: Teen rider accused of stabbing three horses at Las Vegas race was ‘stalker,’ injured horse’s owner says

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: Steve Kornacki explains why we may need to practice patience with the results in California, which is hosting major contests for governor, House and mayor.

media:content.media:text: Two hands hold "I voted" stickers held next to each other
media:content.media:description: Voters hold "I Voted" stickers at the polls during Tuesday's primary election, on June 2, 2026.

item.title: Playing the waiting game in California: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: Wildlife officials in Colorado and Oregon are allowing people to fish as much as as they want at some reservoirs that are expected to run dry because of drought.

media:content.media:credit: Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife
media:content.media:description: The Thief Valley Reservoir in Oregon, pictured in August 2024, is expected to go dry. State officials have removed limits on fishing.

media:content.media:text: Water flows out of the Antero Reservoir and into the South Platte River in Antero Junction
media:content.media:description: The Thief Valley Reservoir in Oregon, pictured in August 2024, is expected to go dry. State officials have removed limits on fishing.

media:content.media:credit: Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife
media:content.media:description: The Thief Valley Reservoir in Oregon, pictured in August 2024, is expected to go dry. State officials have removed limits on fishing.

media:content.media:credit: Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife
media:content.media:description: The Thief Valley Reservoir in Oregon, pictured in August 2024, is expected to go dry. State officials have removed limits on fishing.

media:content.media:text: A sign hangs from a locked gate closing the road to the Antero Reservoir
media:content.media:description: The Thief Valley Reservoir in Oregon, pictured in August 2024, is expected to go dry. State officials have removed limits on fishing.

item.title: The fish will die regardless: As some Western reservoirs run dry, officials lift fishing limits
